evals.prompt: I have local train.tsv and test.tsv files for drug reviews. Load them with Hugging Face datasets, make a reproducible sample, rename an Unnamed: 0 id column, drop rows where condition is missing, lowercase condition, add review_length, and show how to filter out very short reviews.
evals.expected_output: Uses load_dataset('csv', data_files={...}, delimiter='\t'), shuffle(seed).select(range(...)), rename_column, filter before lowercasing, map for normalization and review_length, and returns clear code that preserves train/test splits.

evals.prompt: I need to process a 400GB JSONL dataset from a URL but my laptop cannot store it. Show me how to preview examples, tokenize it, shuffle enough for training, and create train/validation streams without indexing into it.
evals.expected_output: Chooses load_dataset(..., streaming=True), explains IterableDataset limitations, uses next(iter(...)), map(..., batched=True), shuffle(buffer_size=..., seed=...), take(), and skip(), without materializing the full dataset.

evals.prompt: Build a Hugging Face dataset from GitHub issues for my repo and push it to the Hub. Include comments, distinguish pull requests from issues, avoid leaking tokens, and tell me what documentation I need before sharing it.
evals.expected_output: Uses the GitHub API with environment/CLI-managed tokens, handles pagination/rate limits or calls them out, saves JSONL, loads it with load_dataset('json'), adds is_pull_request and comments, pushes with push_to_hub, and requires a dataset card covering provenance, intended use, fields, license/privacy, and bias notes.

evals.prompt: I have a dataset of issue titles, bodies, comments, and URLs. Make a semantic search index so short questions retrieve the most relevant comments. Use Hugging Face datasets and FAISS.
evals.expected_output: Filters noisy rows, explodes or otherwise creates one comment per row, builds a concatenated text column, embeds with a semantic-search-suitable model, adds a FAISS index on embeddings, and queries with get_nearest_examples returning URLs and scores.
